2606.07316.title: Hierarchical Certified Semantic Commitment for Byzantine-Resilient LLM-Agent Collaboration
2606.07316.paper_summary: Byzantine collaboration among large-language-model agents requires a finality-control primitive: given delivered stochastic, structured natural-language proposals, the protocol must decide whether the round supports a commit, what kind of commit, or a typed safe abort. Naive aggregation hides this choice behind a single verdict; classical Byzantine fault tolerance hides it behind byte-identity that LLM proposals do not satisfy. We introduce Hierarchical Certified Semantic Commitment (H-CSC), a BFT-inspired protocol that converts embedding-derived finality signals over verdict-conditioned proposal groups into one of three typed outcomes: a semantic_commit (a 2f+1 within-verdict semantic core backs the verdict, emitting a parameter-bound digest over the quantised aggregate), a verdict_commit (strong verdict margin but dispersed semantic rationale, emitting a verdict-level certificate without claiming a semantic aggregate), or an explicit abort with a typed reason. The contribution is typed finality, not raw commit accuracy. On a controlled semantic-poisoning diagnostic (BCS_v1, 120 episodes), H-CSC commits with low angular deviation on BFT-feasible buckets (0.31 to 2.04 degrees) and aborts 100% of beyond-BFT rounds (n<3f+1) as intended. On a real LLM-agent claim-verification benchmark (MVR-50, 50 tasks) under paired static and rushing Byzantine attacks, H-CSC commits 0.90/0.92 with honest-reference-invalid rates of 0.02/0.00, statistically matching a strong certificate-emitting verdict-only baseline. Unlike that baseline, H-CSC also emits an embedding-backed semantic_commit digest on 74%/72% of rounds, supplying typed provenance. A strict-semantic ablation commits only 0.54/0.48, showing the verdict-level fallback is necessary for coverage (+0.36/+0.44) at the same <=0.04 safety floor; a 100-task cross-model check across four LLMs preserves invalid_hmaj within 0.00 to 0.03.
2606.07316.paper_summary_zh: 拜占庭協作在大型語言模型代理之間需要一個最終性控制原語：在給定的隨機、結構化自然語言提案中，協議必須決定該回合是否支持提交、提交的類型或類型安全的中止。天真的聚合將這個選擇隱藏在單一的裁決之後；傳統的拜占庭容錯將其隱藏在字節身份中，而大型語言模型提案並不滿足這一點。我們引入了層級認證語義承諾（H-CSC），這是一種受BFT啟發的協議，將嵌入衍生的最終性信號轉換為三種類型的結果之一：語義承諾（在裁決內的2f+1語義核心支持該裁決，發出一個參數限制的摘要，基於量化的聚合）、裁決承諾（強裁決邊際但分散的語義理由，發出一個裁決級別的證書而不聲稱語義聚合），或帶有類型原因的明確中止。貢獻是類型最終性，而不是原始提交準確性。在一個控制的語義中毒診斷（BCS_v1，120集）中，H-CSC在BFT可行的桶上以低角度偏差進行提交（0.31到2.04度），並如預期地在超出BFT的回合中中止100%（n<3f+1）。在一個真實的LLM代理索賠驗證基準（MVR-50，50個任務）下，面對配對靜態和急速的拜占庭攻擊，H-CSC以0.90/0.92的提交率和誠實參考無效率0.02/0.00，統計上匹配強證書發出的僅裁決基準。與該基準不同，H-CSC還在74%/72%的回合中發出嵌入支持的語義承諾摘要，提供類型來源。一個嚴格的語義消融僅提交0.54/0.48，顯示裁決級別的回退在同樣的<=0.04安全底線下是必要的以覆蓋（+0.36/+0.44）；一個跨四個LLM的100任務跨模型檢查保持無效_hmaj在0.00到0.03之間。
2606.07316.author: Haoran Xu et.al.
2606.07316.authors: Haoran Xu, Lei Zhang, Iadh Ounis, Xianbin Wang
